| POST | `/menu/manage/item/:id/substitutes` | Set substitutes |
| POST | `/menu/manage/item/:id/choices` | Add choice option |
| POST | `/menu/manage/blackout` | Add holiday blackout |
| POST | `/menu/manage/blackout/:id/delete` | Remove blackout |
| POST | `/menu/manage/promotion` | Add promotion |

### Admin Only

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/admin/users` | User management |
| GET | `/admin/register` | Registration form |
| POST | `/admin/register` | Create user |
| POST | `/admin/users/:id/toggle` | Activate/deactivate |
| POST | `/admin/users/:id/role` | Change role |
| POST | `/admin/users/:id/temp-access` | Grant temporary access |
| GET | `/admin/performance` | Performance dashboard |
| POST | `/admin/refresh-views` | Refresh materialized views |
| GET | `/admin/webhooks` | Webhook management |
| POST | `/admin/webhooks` | Register webhook endpoint |
| GET | `/admin/bookings` | All bookings (admin view) |

### Internal (HMAC-signed)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/api/internal/clinic-utilization` | Clinic report data |
| GET | `/api/internal/booking-fill-rates` | Booking report data |
| GET | `/api/internal/menu-sell-through` | Menu report data |
| POST | `/webhooks/receive` | Inbound webhook receiver |

---

## Security Features

| Feature | Implementation |
|---------|----------------|
| Password hashing | bcrypt with default cost |
| Session tokens | 32 random bytes → 64-char hex string, 24h expiry |
| RBAC | Per-route-group middleware checking user role |
| Data scoping | Students see own data only; clinicians see dept; admin sees org |
| Temporary access | Auto-reverts via background ticker (checked every 1 min) |
| HMAC request signing | SHA-256 HMAC of `method:path:timestamp`, 5-min window |
| Rate limiting | 60 requests/minute per client IP, sliding window |
| File upload validation | Server-side: 10MB max, allowlisted MIME types only |
| File integrity | SHA-256 fingerprint stored for every uploaded file |
| Audit trail | Immutable log with SHA-256 fingerprint per revision |
| PII masking | SSN: `***-**-1234`; Email: `ja***@domain` (role-based) |
| SQL injection | Prevented by GORM parameterized queries |
| XSS | Prevented by Go html/template auto-escaping |

---

## Configuration

All settings are configured via environment variables (see `docker-compose.yml`):

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `DB_HOST` | `localhost` | PostgreSQL host |
| `DB_PORT` | `5432` | PostgreSQL port |
| `DB_USER` | `campus_admin` | Database username |
| `DB_PASSWORD` | `campus_secret` | Database password |
| `DB_NAME` | `campus_portal` | Database name |
| `DB_SSLMODE` | `disable` | SSL mode for DB connection |
| `SERVER_PORT` | `8080` | Web server port |
| `SESSION_KEY` | (set in compose) | Session signing key |
| `HMAC_SECRET` | (set in compose) | HMAC signing key |
| `UPLOAD_DIR` | `./uploads` | File upload storage path |
| `WATCHED_DIR` | `./watched_folder` | CSV import watch path |
| `GIN_MODE` | `release` | Gin framework mode |

---

## Original Prompt

> Implement a Campus Wellness & Training Operations Portal for a university-run health clinic and athletics department that must function entirely on an internal network. Users sign in with a local username and password and land on a Templ-rendered web dashboard tailored by role: students and faculty can review their own health record summary (allergies, conditions, recent vitals) and upload supporting documents such as PDFs or images up to 10 MB with immediate client-side type/size validation and clear success/error feedback. Clinicians (doctors, nurses, lab, pharmacy) use structured, template-driven forms to record encounters and vitals (e.g., weight in lb, blood pressure, temperature) and can switch between department views without losing unsaved changes. Athletics staff and participants book sparring/partner-training sessions through a calendar that shows available 30-minute slots, supports on-site venue rooms or "virtual session" as a location type, and guides matching using configurable criteria like skill level bands, weight classes in lb, and primary style; conflicts are highlighted before submission, and users can cancel up to 2 hours before start. Bookings follow a visible order lifecycle (initiated, confirmed, canceled, refunded) with on-screen status badges and audit notes explaining who changed what and when. For on-campus dining coordination tied to training events, authorized staff maintain an offline restaurant menu with multi-level categories and SKUs for dishes, combos, add-ons, prep and flavor choices; they can set sell windows (e.g., weekdays 6:30 AM–2:00 PM) and holiday blackouts, mark items sold out with substitute suggestions, and apply pricing rules for dine-in vs takeout, member discounts, and time-bound promotions, with totals displayed clearly before a request is saved.
>
> The backend uses Gin to expose REST-style endpoints consumed by the Templ UI, enforcing RBAC on every page action and API call with data scope limited by organization, department, and individual record ownership; administrators can activate/deactivate accounts, change roles, and grant temporary elevated access that expires automatically after a set duration (default 8 hours). PostgreSQL persists all health records, booking orders, and menu/SKU configurations with versioned change history: each record update creates an immutable revision with editor identity, timestamp (MM/DD/YYYY hh:mm AM/PM), and a human-readable reason, while attachments are stored locally on disk with database references, SHA-256 fingerprints, and strict allowlists for file types. Offline "integration" is supported through scheduled imports from on-prem SSO and academic enrollment exports (CSV dropped into a watched folder) to verify eligibility and sync org structure, plus locally hosted webhook receivers and outbound webhook deliveries to other internal systems for competition platforms, e-signature, plagiarism checks, and a data warehouse; all API access uses locally issued tokens, HMAC request signing, and rate limiting (e.g., 60 requests/minute per client) without any external network dependency. High-volume reporting (e.g., clinic utilization, booking fill rates, menu sell-through) is accelerated using PostgreSQL materialized views refreshed on a schedule, partitioning for large time-series vitals by month, result caching with TTL (e.g., 5 minutes) for repeated queries, and async loading for long-running searches; the system records slow queries over 500 ms, surfaces performance dashboards to admins, and encrypts sensitive fields at rest with masked display defaults (e.g., partial SSN if stored) to support HIPAA-aligned privacy in a fully local deployment.
"}

A university-run health clinic and athletics department operations portal designed to function entirely on an internal network. Built with Go (Gin), HTML/CSS/JS, and PostgreSQL.

## Overview

This portal serves as a centralized system for campus wellness operations spanning:

- **Health clinic** management (records, vitals, encounters, document uploads)
- **Athletics** training session booking with partner matching
- **Dining** menu management tied to training events
- **Administration** with role-based access control, audit logging, and performance monitoring

The system is designed for **fully offline/internal network** deployment with no external dependencies.

---

## Tech Stack

| Layer      | Technology                        |
|------------|-----------------------------------|
| Language   | Go 1.26                          |
| Web Server | Gin                               |
| Frontend   | Go html/template + vanilla JS/CSS |
| Database   | PostgreSQL 16                     |
| Auth       | bcrypt + session cookies          |
| Container  | Docker / Docker Compose           |

---

## Quick Start

```bash
docker compose up --build
```

Then open **http://localhost:8080** in your browser.

That single command:
1. Starts a PostgreSQL 16 container with healthcheck
2. Builds the Go binary in a multi-stage Docker build
3. Runs database migrations (GORM AutoMigrate)
4. Seeds default organizations, departments, venues, users, menu items
5. Starts the Gin web server on port 8080

To stop:
```bash
docker compose down
```

To stop and wipe all data (fresh start):
```bash
docker compose down -v
```

---

## Default Login Credentials

All seed accounts use the password **`password123`**

| Username    | Role       | Department       |
|-------------|------------|------------------|
| `admin`     | Admin      | —                |
| `student`   | Student    | —                |
| `faculty`   | Faculty    | —                |
| `clinician` | Clinician  | General Medicine |
| `labtech`   | Clinician  | Laboratory       |
| `staff`     | Staff      | Dining Services  |
| `trainer`   | Staff      | Athletics        |

---

## Project Structure

```
Task-20/
├── cmd/
│   └── server/
│       └── main.go                  # Entry point, router, background workers
├── internal/
│   ├── auth/
│   │   └── auth.go                  # Session auth, login, register, temp access
│   ├── config/
│   │   └── config.go                # Environment-based configuration
│   ├── handlers/
│   │   ├── admin_handlers.go        # Performance dashboard, webhooks, reporting APIs
│   │   ├── auth_handlers.go         # Login/logout pages, user management, temp access
│   │   ├── booking_handlers.go      # Training session booking, slots, partner matching
│   │   ├── health_handlers.go       # Health dashboard, vitals, encounters, file upload
│   │   └── menu_handlers.go         # Menu browsing, management, ordering, pricing
│   ├── middleware/
│   │   └── middleware.go            # AuthRequired, RBAC, DataScope, HMAC, rate limiter
│   ├── models/
│   │   ├── database.go              # DB init with retry, AutoMigrate, seed data
│   │   └── models.go                # All GORM models (27 tables)
│   ├── services/
│   │   ├── audit.go                 # Immutable audit log with SHA-256 fingerprints
│   │   ├── booking.go               # Slot engine, partner matching, conflict detection
│   │   ├── health.go                # Health records, vitals, encounters, file storage
│   │   ├── integration.go           # CSV watcher, webhooks, reporting, PII masking
│   │   └── menu.go                  # Categories, items, pricing engine, sell windows
│   └── templates/
│       ├── funcs.go                 # Custom template functions (divf, deref)
│       ├── layout.html              # Base layout with role-aware navbar
│       ├── login.html               # Login page
│       ├── dashboard.html           # Health dashboard (vitals, records, uploads)
│       ├── clinician.html           # Clinician encounter/vitals forms with draft save
│       ├── bookings.html            # Training session booking with calendar
│       ├── bookings_admin.html      # Admin view of all bookings
│       ├── menu.html                # Dining menu with ordering
│       ├── menu_manage.html         # Menu management (categories, items, sell windows)
│       ├── admin_users.html         # User management (activate, roles, temp access)
│       ├── admin_performance.html   # Performance dashboard (slow queries, reports)
│       ├── admin_webhooks.html      # Webhook endpoint management
│       ├── register.html            # New user registration form
│       └── error.html               # Error display page
├── static/
│   ├── css/
│   │   └── style.css                # Complete responsive stylesheet
│   └── js/
│       └── app.js                   # Client-side validation, drafts, AJAX helpers
├── migrations/

├── db-init/

├── uploads/                         # Runtime: uploaded documents stored here
├── watched_folder/                  # Runtime: CSV drop folder for enrollment imports
├── Dockerfile                       # Multi-stage Go build

├── .dockerignore                    # Build context exclusions
├── go.mod                           # Go module definition
└── go.sum                           # Dependency checksums
```

---

## Architecture

```
┌─────────────┐     ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│   Browser    │────▶│              Gin Router (port 8080)          │
│  (HTML/CSS/  │◀────│                                              │
│   JS)        │     │  Middleware Stack:                           │
└─────────────┘     │  ├─ Rate Limiter (60 req/min per IP)        │
                     │  ├─ HMAC Auth (internal API routes)         │
                     │  ├─ Session Auth (cookie-based)             │
                     │  ├─ RBAC (role check per route group)       │
                     │  └─ Data Scope (self/dept/org filtering)    │
                     │                                              │
                     │  Route Groups:                               │
                     │  ├─ Public: /login                          │
                     │  ├─ Authed: /dashboard, /bookings, /menu    │
                     │  ├─ Clinician: /clinician/*                 │
                     │  ├─ Staff: /menu/manage/*                   │
                     │  ├─ Admin: /admin/*                         │
                     │  └─ Internal API: /api/internal/* (HMAC)    │
                     └──────────────┬───────────────────────────────┘
                                    │
                     ┌──────────────▼───────────────────────────────┐
                     │           Service Layer                      │
                     │  ├─ AuthService     (sessions, temp access)  │
                     │  ├─ HealthService   (records, vitals, files) │
                     │  ├─ BookingService  (slots, matching, FSM)   │
                     │  ├─ MenuService     (pricing, sell windows)  │
                     │  ├─ AuditService    (immutable revisions)    │
                     │  ├─ WebhookService  (dispatch + delivery)    │
                     │  ├─ ReportingService(mat views, cache)       │
                     │  └─ CSVWatcher      (folder polling)         │
                     └──────────────┬───────────────────────────────┘
                                    │
                     ┌──────────────▼───────────────────────────────┐
                     │         PostgreSQL 16 (GORM)                 │
                     │  27 tables + 3 materialized views            │
                     │  Indexes on all foreign keys & query paths   │
                     └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Background Workers (goroutines):**
- Temp access revert ticker (every 1 minute)
- Materialized view refresh ticker (every 15 minutes)
- CSV watched folder poller (every 10 seconds)

## Feature Inventory

---

## Database Schema

### Tables (27)

| Table | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `organizations` | Top-level org (multi-tenant support) |
| `departments` | Departments within an organization |
| `users` | All user accounts with role + org + dept |
| `sessions` | Active login sessions (64-char token) |
| `temp_accesses` | Temporary elevated role grants with expiry |
| `health_records` | Per-user health summary (allergies, conditions, meds) |
| `vitals` | Time-series vitals (weight lb, BP, temp F, HR) |
| `encounters` | Clinician encounter records by department |
| `attachments` | Uploaded file metadata + SHA-256 + disk path |
| `audit_logs` | Immutable revision history for all record changes |
| `venues` | Training venues (onsite rooms + virtual) |
| `trainer_profiles` | Skill level, weight class (lb), primary style |
| `bookings` | Training session bookings with state machine |
| `booking_audits` | Booking state change audit trail |
| `menu_categories` | Nested menu categories with sort order |
| `menu_items` | SKU-based items with dual pricing + member discount |
| `menu_item_choices` | Prep, flavor, size options per item |
| `item_substitutes` | Many-to-many sold-out substitution suggestions |
| `sell_windows` | Day-of-week + time range availability per item |
| `holiday_blackouts` | Date-based blackout days |
| `promotions` | Time-bound percentage discounts per item |
| `menu_orders` | Placed orders (dine-in/takeout, member flag) |
| `menu_order_items` | Line items per order with computed unit price |
| `webhook_endpoints` | Registered outbound webhook targets |
| `webhook_deliveries` | Delivery attempt log per endpoint |
| `slow_query_logs` | Queries exceeding 500ms threshold |

### Materialized Views (3)

| View | Purpose | Refresh |
|------|---------|---------|
| `mv_clinic_utilization` | Encounters per day per department | Every 15 min |
| `mv_booking_fill_rates` | Bookings per day per venue (confirmed/canceled) | Every 15 min |
| `mv_menu_sell_through` | Items sold + revenue per SKU | Every 15 min |

## API Endpoints

---

## Security Features

| Feature | Implementation |
|---------|----------------|
| Password hashing | bcrypt with default cost |
| Session tokens | 32 random bytes → 64-char hex string, 24h expiry |
| RBAC | Per-route-group middleware checking user role |
| Data scoping | Students see own data only; clinicians see dept; admin sees org |
| Temporary access | Auto-reverts via background ticker (checked every 1 min) |
| HMAC request signing | SHA-256 HMAC of `method:path:timestamp`, 5-min window |
| Rate limiting | 60 requests/minute per client IP, sliding window |
| File upload validation | Server-side: 10MB max, allowlisted MIME types only |
| File integrity | SHA-256 fingerprint stored for every uploaded file |
| Audit trail | Immutable log with SHA-256 fingerprint per revision |
| PII masking | SSN: `***-**-1234`; Email: `ja***@domain` (role-based) |
| SQL injection | Prevented by GORM parameterized queries |
| XSS | Prevented by Go html/template auto-escaping |

---

## Configuration

All settings are configured via environment variables (see `docker-compose.yml`):

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `DB_HOST` | `localhost` | PostgreSQL host |
| `DB_PORT` | `5432` | PostgreSQL port |
| `DB_USER` | `campus_admin` | Database username |
| `DB_PASSWORD` | `campus_secret` | Database password |
| `DB_NAME` | `campus_portal` | Database name |
| `DB_SSLMODE` | `disable` | SSL mode for DB connection |
| `SERVER_PORT` | `8080` | Web server port |
| `SESSION_KEY` | (set in compose) | Session signing key |
| `HMAC_SECRET` | (set in compose) | HMAC signing key |
| `UPLOAD_DIR` | `./uploads` | File upload storage path |
| `WATCHED_DIR` | `./watched_folder` | CSV import watch path |
| `GIN_MODE` | `release` | Gin framework mode |

---

## Original Prompt

> Implement a Campus Wellness & Training Operations Portal for a university-run health clinic and athletics department that must function entirely on an internal network. Users sign in with a local username and password and land on a Templ-rendered web dashboard tailored by role: students and faculty can review their own health record summary (allergies, conditions, recent vitals) and upload supporting documents such as PDFs or images up to 10 MB with immediate client-side type/size validation and clear success/error feedback. Clinicians (doctors, nurses, lab, pharmacy) use structured, template-driven forms to record encounters and vitals (e.g., weight in lb, blood pressure, temperature) and can switch between department views without losing unsaved changes. Athletics staff and participants book sparring/partner-training sessions through a calendar that shows available 30-minute slots, supports on-site venue rooms or "virtual session" as a location type, and guides matching using configurable criteria like skill level bands, weight classes in lb, and primary style; conflicts are highlighted before submission, and users can cancel up to 2 hours before start. Bookings follow a visible order lifecycle (initiated, confirmed, canceled, refunded) with on-screen status badges and audit notes explaining who changed what and when. For on-campus dining coordination tied to training events, authorized staff maintain an offline restaurant menu with multi-level categories and SKUs for dishes, combos, add-ons, prep and flavor choices; they can set sell windows (e.g., weekdays 6:30 AM–2:00 PM) and holiday blackouts, mark items sold out with substitute suggestions, and apply pricing rules for dine-in vs takeout, member discounts, and time-bound promotions, with totals displayed clearly before a request is saved.
>
> The backend uses Gin to expose REST-style endpoints consumed by the Templ UI, enforcing RBAC on every page action and API call with data scope limited by organization, department, and individual record ownership; administrators can activate/deactivate accounts, change roles, and grant temporary elevated access that expires automatically after a set duration (default 8 hours). PostgreSQL persists all health records, booking orders, and menu/SKU configurations with versioned change history: each record update creates an immutable revision with editor identity, timestamp (MM/DD/YYYY hh:mm AM/PM), and a human-readable reason, while attachments are stored locally on disk with database references, SHA-256 fingerprints, and strict allowlists for file types. Offline "integration" is supported through scheduled imports from on-prem SSO and academic enrollment exports (CSV dropped into a watched folder) to verify eligibility and sync org structure, plus locally hosted webhook receivers and outbound webhook deliveries to other internal systems for competition platforms, e-signature, plagiarism checks, and a data warehouse; all API access uses locally issued tokens, HMAC request signing, and rate limiting (e.g., 60 requests/minute per client) without any external network dependency. High-volume reporting (e.g., clinic utilization, booking fill rates, menu sell-through) is accelerated using PostgreSQL materialized views refreshed on a schedule, partitioning for large time-series vitals by month, result caching with TTL (e.g., 5 minutes) for repeated queries, and async loading for long-running searches; the system records slow queries over 500 ms, surfaces performance dashboards to admins, and encrypts sensitive fields at rest with masked display defaults (e.g., partial SSN if stored) to support HIPAA-aligned privacy in a fully local deployment.
"}

content.text: File created successfully at: C:/Users/amana/OneDrive/Desktop/Files/Eaglepoint/April 1/Task-20/README.md

Created `README.md` with complete documentation covering:

- Quick start (`docker compose up --build`)
- All 7 default login credentials
- Full project structure tree (35 files)
- ASCII architecture diagram with middleware stack + service layer + background workers
- Feature inventory tables for all 5 phases with status and implementation details
- All 27 database tables + 3 materialized views
- Complete API endpoint reference (40+ routes) organized by role
- Security features summary
- Environment variable configuration reference
- The original prompt verbatim at the bottom

got these issues from the backend : ### Finding 1: Zero Test Coverage - **Severity:** Blocker - **Conclusion:** No test files exist anywhere in the project - **Evidence:** `Glob("**/*_test.go")` returns zero results. No `_test.go` files in any package. - **Impact:** No verification of business logic (booking state transitions, pricing calculations, conflict detection, cancellation rules, RBAC enforcement, file validation). Critical for a HIPAA-adjacent system. - **Fix:** Add unit tests for at minimum: `services/booking.go` (state machine, 2-hour rule, conflict detection), `services/menu.go` (pricing/sell-window logic), `auth/auth.go` (login, session validation, temp access), and `middleware/middleware.go` (RBAC, HMAC, scope enforcement). ### Finding 2: No At-Rest Encryption of Sensitive Fields - **Severity:** High - **Conclusion:** The prompt requires "encrypts sensitive fields at rest" for HIPAA-aligned privacy. SSN and health records are stored as plaintext in the database. - **Evidence:** `models.go:67` — `SSN string gorm:"size:255"` is a plain string column. `HealthRecord` fields (`Allergies`, `Conditions`, `Medications`) at lines 93-98 are plain `text` columns. `MaskSSN()` in `integration.go:360-373` only masks display output, not storage. - **Impact:** Fails the prompt's explicit HIPAA-aligned encryption requirement. Any database dump exposes raw PII/PHI. - **Fix:** Implement application-level AES-256 encryption for SSN, health record fields, and encounter notes before DB write, with decryption on read. Use a key management approach (e.g., env-injected key). ### Finding 3: File Upload Endpoint Missing Authorization Scope Check - **Severity:** High - **Conclusion:** Any authenticated user can upload files to any other user's health record. - **Evidence:** `health_handlers.go:111-115` — `UploadAttachment` reads `user_id` from form data and uses it directly. Unlike `UpdateHealthRecord` (line 59-62) and `DashboardPage` (line 29-33), which call `middleware.EnforceSelfScope()`, `UploadAttachment` never calls it. A student could POST `user_id=` and attach files to the admin's record. - **Impact:** Object-level authorization bypass. Violates the prompt's requirement that data scope is "limited by organization, department, and individual record ownership." - **Fix:** Add `if !middleware.EnforceSelfScope(c, targetUserID)` check before proceeding with the upload, returning 403 on failure. ### Finding 4: Audit/History Endpoints Lack Authorization - **Severity:** High - **Conclusion:** Two endpoints expose audit data to any authenticated user without scope checks. - **Evidence:** - `health_handlers.go:198-209` — `RecordHistory` accepts arbitrary `table` and `record_id` query params with no RBAC or scope check. Any authenticated user can query audit logs for any table/record. - `booking_handlers.go:163-171` — `BookingAudit` returns audit trail for any booking ID without checking if the requester owns or has access to that booking. - **Impact:** Information disclosure of PHI change history and booking activity across users. - **Fix:** Validate that the current user has access to the referenced record before returning audit data. ### Finding 5: Webhook Receiver Endpoint Unauthenticated - **Severity:** Medium - **Conclusion:** `/webhooks/receive` is registered on the root router with no authentication. - **Evidence:** `main.go:166-175` — `r.POST("/webhooks/receive", ...)` is on `r` (root), not on `authed`. The HMAC middleware only enforces on `/api/internal/*` prefix (`middleware.go:120`), so this endpoint has no auth. - **Impact:** Any network client can POST arbitrary JSON payloads to this endpoint. While it only logs the payload currently, it creates an open ingestion point. - **Fix:** Either move under the HMAC-protected `/api/internal/` prefix, or add explicit HMAC/token validation for this route. ### Finding 6: No Non-Docker Run Instructions - **Severity:** Medium - **Conclusion:** The README only documents `docker compose up --build`. No instructions for running locally with a standalone PostgreSQL instance. - **Evidence:** `README.md:57-59` — Quick Start section only shows Docker commands. The config (`config.go:34-56`) does support env vars for a local PostgreSQL, but this is undocumented. - **Impact:** Reduces portability and testability. Users without Docker cannot verify the project. - **Fix:** Add a "Local Development" section to the README documenting: (1) PostgreSQL setup, (2) env vars to set, (3) `go run ./cmd/server` command. ### Finding 7: Templ Library Not Used (Uses html/template Instead) - **Severity:** Medium - **Conclusion:** The prompt specifies "Templ-rendered web dashboard." The project uses Go's standard `html/template` package, not the `a-h/templ` library. - **Evidence:** `cmd/server/main.go:69-74` — uses `template.Must(template.New("").Funcs(...).ParseGlob(...))`. `go.mod` has no `github.com/a-h/templ` dependency. `Glob("**/*.templ")` returns zero files. - **Impact:** Deviation from a specific prompt technology requirement. The functional outcome (server-rendered HTML templates) is equivalent, but the tooling choice doesn't match. - **Fix:** Either migrate templates to `.templ` files using the Templ library, or document the rationale for using `html/template` as an equivalent approach. ### Finding 8: Vitals Partitioning Not Implemented - **Severity:** Low - **Conclusion:** The prompt requires "partitioning for large time-series vitals by month." The code only creates an index. - **Evidence:** `database.go:85-91` — The comment says "Create vitals partitioning" but the SQL only creates `idx_vitals_recorded_at` index. No `PARTITION BY RANGE` or monthly partition creation exists. GORM's `AutoMigrate` creates a regular (non-partitioned) table. - **Impact:** Performance requirement unmet for large vitals datasets. Functional impact is low at small scale. - **Fix:** Implement PostgreSQL native range partitioning by month on the vitals table (requires raw DDL, not GORM AutoMigrate). and these from the frontend: ### Finding 2: Zero Automated Tests - **Severity:** Blocker - **Conclusion:** No unit tests, component tests, integration tests, or E2E tests exist anywhere in the project. - **Evidence:** No `*_test.go` files found across the entire repository. No test framework imports in `go.mod`. - **Impact:** No automated verification of business logic correctness (booking FSM, pricing engine, conflict detection, RBAC, 2-hour cancellation rule, sell windows, etc.). - **Fix:** Add test files at minimum for: (1) booking state machine transitions, (2) pricing engine with discounts/promotions, (3) RBAC middleware, (4) file upload validation, (5) conflict detection logic. ### Finding 3: Hardcoded Secrets in Default Configuration - **Severity:** High - **Conclusion:** Session key and HMAC secret have hardcoded fallback values in source code and are repeated in `docker-compose.yml`. - **Evidence:** `config/config.go:43-44` — `SessionKey: "super-secret-session-key-change-me-32b!"`, `HMACSecret: "hmac-secret-key-for-internal-apis"`. Same values in `docker-compose.yml:30-31`. - **Impact:** If deployed without changing environment variables, the session signing and HMAC authentication use publicly known secrets. While the prompt specifies internal-network deployment, this remains a security concern for HIPAA-aligned systems. - **Fix:** Remove hardcoded fallbacks; require these values via environment variables or fail on startup if absent. ### Finding 4: Session Cookie Missing Secure Flag Management - **Severity:** Medium - **Conclusion:** Session cookie is set with `Secure: false` hardcoded, and `HttpOnly: true`, but there is no CSRF protection mechanism. - **Evidence:** `handlers/auth_handlers.go:46` — `c.SetCookie("session_id", sessionID, 86400, "/", "", false, true)`. All state-changing operations use POST forms without CSRF tokens. - **Impact:** Forms are vulnerable to cross-site request forgery. On an internal network this is lower risk but still a gap for a HIPAA-aligned system. - **Fix:** Implement CSRF token middleware (e.g., `gin-csrf` or custom token-per-session). ### Finding 5: Upload Handler Missing Scope Enforcement - **Severity:** Medium - **Conclusion:** The `UploadAttachment` handler accepts a `user_id` from the form but does not enforce `DataScope`/`EnforceSelfScope` on who can upload to another user's record. - **Evidence:** `handlers/health_handlers.go:112-114` — `targetUserID` is set from form input without scope check (unlike `UpdateHealthRecord` on line 60-63 which does check). - **Impact:** A student could potentially upload attachments to another user's record by manipulating the `user_id` form field. - **Fix:** Add `middleware.EnforceSelfScope(c, targetUserID)` check before processing the upload. ### Finding 6: Prompt Specifies Templ Templating — Project Uses html/template - **Severity:** Medium - **Conclusion:** The prompt explicitly states "Templ-rendered web dashboard" but the project uses Go's standard `html/template` library instead of the Templ templating framework. - **Evidence:** `cmd/server/main.go:4` imports `html/template`. Templates are `.html` files, not `.templ` files. No `templ` dependency in `go.mod`. - **Impact:** Deviation from prompt specification on technology choice. The functional outcome (server-rendered HTML) is equivalent, but the specific technology was not followed. - **Fix:** Acknowledged deviation; functionally equivalent. No action strictly required unless prompt compliance is enforced literally. ### Finding 7: `recalculateAll()` Function Is a Stub - **Severity:** Medium - **Conclusion:** The menu page's price recalculation on order type/member change does not actually work. - **Evidence:** `static/js/app.js:368-371` — `function recalculateAll() { // In a full implementation, this would re-fetch prices via API }`. - **Impact:** When a user toggles dine-in/takeout or member discount on the menu page, displayed prices do not update. This breaks the prompt requirement that "totals displayed clearly before a request is saved." - **Fix:** Implement the function to call `/api/price` for each item in the order and update the display. ### Finding 8: Order Total Not Computed on Client Side - **Severity:** Medium - **Conclusion:** The `orderTotal` span is never populated with actual prices. The `updateOrderSummary()` function shows items but doesn't calculate or display the running total. - **Evidence:** `static/js/app.js:347-358` — `updateOrderSummary` builds item display HTML but never fetches prices or updates `orderTotal`. `menu.html:91` has `$0.00` that remains at 0.00. - **Impact:** Users cannot see the order total before submission, violating the prompt requirement for clear total display. - **Fix:** Fetch prices via `/api/price` API within `addToOrder()` and maintain a running total. ### Finding 9: Vitals Partitioning Claimed But Not Implemented - **Severity:** Low - **Conclusion:** The prompt requires "partitioning for large time-series vitals by month." The code only creates an index, not actual table partitioning. - **Evidence:** `models/database.go:85-91` — Creates `idx_vitals_recorded_at` index with a comment about partitioning, but no `PARTITION BY RANGE` DDL is executed. GORM `AutoMigrate` creates a regular table. - **Impact:** Performance optimization for time-series data is missing. Functionally correct but does not meet the partitioning requirement. - **Fix:** Implement PostgreSQL native range partitioning on the vitals table by month. ### Finding 10: Sensitive Field Encryption at Rest Not Implemented - **Severity:** Low - **Conclusion:** The prompt requires "encrypts sensitive fields at rest." The SSN field is stored as plaintext in the database. PII masking exists only at display time. - **Evidence:** `models/models.go:67` — `SSN string gorm:"size:255"` is a plain string column. `integration.go:360-373` — `MaskSSN` only masks for display, does not decrypt. - **Impact:** Health data compliance gap. SSN and other sensitive fields are stored unencrypted in PostgreSQL. - **Fix:** Use `pgcrypto` (already installed via `db-init/01-extensions.sql`) to encrypt sensitive columns at rest. fix all

118		file, err := c.FormFile("file")
119		if err != nil {
120			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "no file provided"})
121			return
122		}
123	
124		// Server-side validation (client-side also validates)
125		if file.Size > 10*1024*1024 {
126			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "file exceeds 10MB limit"})
127			return
128		}
129	
130		contentType := file.Header.Get("Content-Type")
131		allowed := map[string]bool{
132			"application/pdf": true,
133			"image/jpeg":      true,
134			"image/png":       true,
135			"image/gif":       true,
136		}
137		if !allowed[contentType] {
138			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "file type not allowed. Only PDF, JPEG, PNG, GIF accepted"})
139			return
140		}
141	
142		src, err := file.Open()
143		if err != nil {
144			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": "failed to read file"})
145			return
146		}
147		defer src.Close()
148	
149		attachment, err := h.HealthSvc.SaveAttachment(file, src, targetUserID, user.ID)
150		if err != nil {
151			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
152			return
153		}
154	
155		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{
156			"message":    "File uploaded successfully",
157			"attachment": attachment,
158		})
159	}

32		bookingSvc := services.NewBookingService(db, auditSvc)
33		menuSvc := services.NewMenuService(db, auditSvc)
34		webhookSvc := services.NewWebhookService(db)
35		reportingSvc := services.NewReportingService(db, cfg.CacheTTL)
36	
37		// Handlers
38		authHandler := handlers.NewAuthHandler(authSvc, db, cfg)
39		healthHandler := handlers.NewHealthHandler(healthSvc, auditSvc)
40		bookingHandler := handlers.NewBookingHandler(bookingSvc, db)
41		menuHandler := handlers.NewMenuHandler(menuSvc)
42		adminHandler := handlers.NewAdminHandler(reportingSvc, webhookSvc)
43	
44		// Background workers
45		csvWatcher := services.NewCSVWatcher(db, cfg.WatchedDir)
46		csvWatcher.Start()
47		defer csvWatcher.Stop()
48	
49		// Temp access revert ticker
50		go func() {
51			ticker := time.NewTicker(1 * time.Minute)
52			defer ticker.Stop()
53			for range ticker.C {
54				authSvc.RevertExpiredAccess()
55			}
56		}()
57	
58		// Materialized view refresh ticker
59		go func() {
60			ticker := time.NewTicker(cfg.MVRefreshInterval)
61			defer ticker.Stop()
62			for range ticker.C {
63				reportingSvc.RefreshMaterializedViews()
64			}
65		}()
66	
67		// Router
68		r := gin.Default()
69		htmlTemplates := template.Must(
70			template.New("").Funcs(tmpl.FuncMap()).ParseGlob(
71				filepath.Join("internal", "templates", "*.html"),
72			),
73		)
74		r.SetHTMLTemplate(htmlTemplates)
75		r.Static("/static", "./static")
76		r.MaxMultipartMemory = cfg.MaxUploadMB << 20
77	
78		// Global middleware
79		r.Use(middleware.RateLimit(cfg.RateLimitPerMin))
80		r.Use(middleware.HMACAuth(cfg.HMACSecret))
81		r.Use(middleware.SlowQueryLogger(db, cfg.SlowQueryMs))
82	
83		// Public routes
84		r.GET("/login", authHandler.LoginPage)
85		r.POST("/login", authHandler.Login)
86	
87		// Authenticated routes
88		authed := r.Group("/")
89		authed.Use(middleware.AuthRequired(authSvc))
90		authed.Use(middleware.DataScope())
91		{
92			authed.GET("/", func(c *gin.Context) { c.Redirect(302, "/dashboard") })
93			authed.GET("/logout", authHandler.Logout)
94	
95			// Dashboard - all roles
96			authed.GET("/dashboard", healthHandler.DashboardPage)
97			authed.POST("/health/update", healthHandler.UpdateHealthRecord)
98			authed.POST("/health/upload", healthHandler.UploadAttachment)
99			authed.GET("/health/history", healthHandler.RecordHistory)
100	
101			// Clinician routes
102			clinician := authed.Group("/clinician")
103			clinician.Use(middleware.RequireRole(models.RoleClinician, models.RoleAdmin))
104			{
105				clinician.GET("", healthHandler.ClinicianPage)
106				clinician.POST("/encounter", healthHandler.CreateEncounter)
107				clinician.POST("/vitals", healthHandler.RecordVitals)
108			}
109	
110			// Booking routes - students, faculty, staff, admin
111			authed.GET("/bookings", bookingHandler.BookingPage)
112			authed.POST("/bookings", bookingHandler.CreateBooking)
113			authed.POST("/bookings/:id/transition", bookingHandler.TransitionBooking)
114			authed.GET("/bookings/:id/audit", bookingHandler.BookingAudit)
115			authed.GET("/api/slots", bookingHandler.GetSlots)
116			authed.GET("/api/match-partners", bookingHandler.MatchPartners)
117			authed.GET("/api/check-conflicts", bookingHandler.CheckConflicts)
118	
119			// Menu routes - all roles can view
120			authed.GET("/menu", menuHandler.MenuPage)
121			authed.POST("/menu/order", menuHandler.CreateOrder)
122			authed.GET("/api/price", menuHandler.CalculatePrice)
123	
124			// Menu management - staff and admin only
125			menuManage := authed.Group("/menu/manage")
126			menuManage.Use(middleware.RequireRole(models.RoleStaff, models.RoleAdmin))
127			{
128				menuManage.GET("", menuHandler.MenuManagePage)
129				menuManage.POST("/category", menuHandler.CreateCategory)
130				menuManage.POST("/item", menuHandler.CreateMenuItem)
131				menuManage.POST("/item/:id/sold-out", menuHandler.ToggleSoldOut)
132				menuManage.POST("/item/:id/sell-windows", menuHandler.SetSellWindows)
133				menuManage.POST("/item/:id/substitutes", menuHandler.SetSubstitutes)
134				menuManage.POST("/item/:id/choices", menuHandler.AddChoice)
135				menuManage.POST("/blackout", menuHandler.CreateBlackout)
136				menuManage.POST("/blackout/:id/delete", menuHandler.DeleteBlackout)
137				menuManage.POST("/promotion", menuHandler.CreatePromotion)
138			}
139	
140			// Admin routes
141			admin := authed.Group("/admin")
142			admin.Use(middleware.RequireRole(models.RoleAdmin))
143			{
144				admin.GET("/users", authHandler.UsersPage)
145				admin.GET("/register", authHandler.RegisterPage)
146				admin.POST("/register", authHandler.Register)
147				admin.POST("/users/:id/toggle", authHandler.ToggleUser)
148				admin.POST("/users/:id/role", authHandler.ChangeRole)
149				admin.POST("/users/:id/temp-access", authHandler.GrantTempAccess)
150				admin.GET("/performance", adminHandler.PerformancePage)
151				admin.POST("/refresh-views", adminHandler.RefreshViews)
152				admin.GET("/webhooks", adminHandler.WebhooksPage)
153				admin.POST("/webhooks", adminHandler.RegisterWebhook)
154				admin.GET("/bookings", bookingHandler.AllBookingsPage)
155			}
156	
157			// Internal API routes (HMAC-signed)
158			internal := r.Group("/api/internal")
159			{
160				internal.GET("/clinic-utilization", adminHandler.APIClinicUtilization)
161				internal.GET("/booking-fill-rates", adminHandler.APIBookingFillRates)
162				internal.GET("/menu-sell-through", adminHandler.APIMenuSellThrough)
163			}
164	
165			// Webhook receiver
166			r.POST("/webhooks/receive", func(c *gin.Context) {
167				// Generic webhook receiver for internal systems
168				var payload map[string]interface{}
169				if err := c.BindJSON(&payload); err != nil {
170					c.JSON(400, gin.H{"error": "invalid payload"})
171					return
172				}
173				log.Printf("Webhook received: %v", payload)
174				c.JSON(200, gin.H{"status": "received"})
175			})
176		}
177	
178		log.Printf("Campus Portal starting on :%s", cfg.ServerPort)
179		if err := r.Run(":" + cfg.ServerPort); err != nil {
180			log.Fatalf("Server failed: %v", err)
181		}
182	}

12		"gorm.io/gorm"
13		"gorm.io/gorm/logger"
14	)

16	func InitDB(cfg *config.Config) *gorm.DB {
17		dsn := fmt.Sprintf(
18			"host=%s port=%s user=%s password=%s dbname=%s sslmode=%s",
19			cfg.DBHost, cfg.DBPort, cfg.DBUser, cfg.DBPassword, cfg.DBName, cfg.DBSSLMode,
20		)
21	
22		var db *gorm.DB
23		var err error
24	
25		// Retry loop — wait for Postgres to become ready (critical for Docker Compose)
26		for i := 0; i < 30; i++ {
27			db, err = gorm.Open(postgres.Open(dsn), &gorm.Config{
28				Logger: logger.Default.LogMode(logger.Warn),
29			})
30			if err == nil {
31				sqlDB, _ := db.DB()
32				if pingErr := sqlDB.Ping(); pingErr == nil {
33					break
34				}
35			}
36			log.Printf("Waiting for database to be ready... (%d/30)", i+1)
37			time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
38		}
39		if err != nil {
40			log.Fatalf("Failed to connect to database after retries: %v", err)
41		}
42	
43		sqlDB, _ := db.DB()
44		sqlDB.SetMaxOpenConns(25)
45		sqlDB.SetMaxIdleConns(10)
46		sqlDB.SetConnMaxLifetime(5 * time.Minute)
47	
48		return db
49	}
50	
51	func AutoMigrate(db *gorm.DB) {
52		err := db.AutoMigrate(
53			&Organization{},
54			&DepartmentRecord{},
55			&User{},
56			&TempAccess{},
57			&Session{},
58			&HealthRecord{},
59			&Vital{},
60			&Encounter{},
61			&Attachment{},
62			&AuditLog{},
63			&Venue{},
64			&TrainerProfile{},
65			&Booking{},
66			&BookingAudit{},
67			&MenuCategory{},
68			&MenuItem{},
69			&MenuItemChoice{},
70			&ItemSubstitute{},
71			&SellWindow{},
72			&HolidayBlackout{},
73			&Promotion{},
74			&MenuOrder{},
75			&MenuOrderItem{},
76			&WebhookEndpoint{},
77			&WebhookDelivery{},
78			&SlowQueryLog{},
79		)
80		if err != nil {
81			log.Fatalf("AutoMigrate failed: %v", err)
82		}
83	
84		// Create vitals partitioning (run raw SQL for month-based partitioning)
85		db.Exec(`
86			DO $$ BEGIN
87				IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_class WHERE relname = 'idx_vitals_recorded_at') THEN
88					CREATE INDEX idx_vitals_recorded_at ON vitals (recorded_at);
89				END IF;
90			END $$;
91		`)
92	
93		// Create materialized views for reporting
94		db.Exec(`
95			CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS mv_clinic_utilization AS
96			SELECT
97				date_trunc('day', e.encounter_date) AS day,
98				e.department,
99				COUNT(*) AS encounter_count
100			FROM encounters e
101			GROUP BY day, e.department
102			ORDER BY day DESC;
103		`)
104	
105		db.Exec(`
106			CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS mv_booking_fill_rates AS
107			SELECT
108				date_trunc('day', b.slot_start) AS day,
109				b.venue_id,
110				COUNT(*) AS total_bookings,
111				COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE b.status = 'confirmed') AS confirmed,
112				COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE b.status = 'canceled') AS canceled
113			FROM bookings b
114			GROUP BY day, b.venue_id
115			ORDER BY day DESC;
116		`)
117	
118		db.Exec(`
119			CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS mv_menu_sell_through AS
120			SELECT
121				mi.sku,
122				mi.name,
123				COALESCE(SUM(moi.quantity), 0) AS total_sold,
124				COALESCE(SUM(moi.quantity * moi.unit_price), 0) AS total_revenue
125			FROM menu_items mi
126			LEFT JOIN menu_order_items moi ON moi.menu_item_id = mi.id
127			GROUP BY mi.id, mi.sku, mi.name
128			ORDER BY total_sold DESC;
129		`)
130	
131		// Create audit log trigger function
132		db.Exec(`
133			CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION create_unique_index_if_not_exists(idx_name text, tbl text, col text)
134			RETURNS void AS $$
135			BEGIN
136				IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_class WHERE relname = idx_name) THEN
137					EXECUTE format('CREATE UNIQUE INDEX %I ON %I (%s)', idx_name, tbl, col);
138				END IF;
139			END $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
140		`)
141	
142		log.Println("Database migration completed successfully")
143	}

{"file_path": "C:/Users/amana/OneDrive/Desktop/Files/Eaglepoint/April 1/Task-20/repo/internal/services/booking_test.go", "content": "package services

import (
	"testing"
	"time"

	"campus-portal/internal/models"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
	"gorm.io/driver/sqlite"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

func setupTestDB(t *testing.T) *gorm.DB {
	t.Helper()
	db, err := gorm.Open(sqlite.Open(":memory:"), &gorm.Config{})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	err = db.AutoMigrate(
		&models.Venue{},
		&models.Booking{},
		&models.BookingAudit{},
		&models.TrainerProfile{},
		&models.AuditLog{},
	)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Seed venues
	db.Create(&models.Venue{Name: "Room A", RoomType: "onsite", Capacity: 10})
	db.Create(&models.Venue{Name: "Virtual", RoomType: "virtual", Capacity: 100})

	return db
}

func TestTransitionBooking_ValidTransitions(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewBookingService(db, audit)

	slotStart := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)
	booking, err := svc.CreateBooking(1, nil, 1, slotStart, 1)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, models.BookingInitiated, booking.Status)

	// Initiated -> Confirmed
	err = svc.TransitionBooking(booking.ID, models.BookingConfirmed, 1, "confirming")
	assert.NoError(t, err)

	var updated models.Booking
	db.First(&updated, booking.ID)
	assert.Equal(t, models.BookingConfirmed, updated.Status)

	// Confirmed -> Canceled
	err = svc.TransitionBooking(booking.ID, models.BookingCanceled, 1, "canceling")
	assert.NoError(t, err)

	db.First(&updated, booking.ID)
	assert.Equal(t, models.BookingCanceled, updated.Status)

	// Canceled -> Refunded
	err = svc.TransitionBooking(booking.ID, models.BookingRefunded, 1, "refunding")
	assert.NoError(t, err)

	db.First(&updated, booking.ID)
	assert.Equal(t, models.BookingRefunded, updated.Status)
}

func TestTransitionBooking_InvalidTransitions(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewBookingService(db, audit)

	slotStart := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)
	booking, _ := svc.CreateBooking(1, nil, 1, slotStart, 1)

	// Initiated -> Refunded (invalid — must go through Canceled first)
	err := svc.TransitionBooking(booking.ID, models.BookingRefunded, 1, "skip")
	assert.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "invalid state transition")

	// Confirm it, then try Confirmed -> Initiated (backwards)
	svc.TransitionBooking(booking.ID, models.BookingConfirmed, 1, "ok")
	err = svc.TransitionBooking(booking.ID, models.BookingInitiated, 1, "backwards")
	assert.Error(t, err)
}

func TestTransitionBooking_TwoHourCancellationRule(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewBookingService(db, audit)

	// Create a booking that starts in 1 hour (less than 2 hours)
	slotStart := time.Now().Add(1 * time.Hour)
	booking := &models.Booking{
		RequesterID: 1,
		VenueID:     1,
		SlotStart:   slotStart,
		SlotEnd:     slotStart.Add(30 * time.Minute),
		Status:      models.BookingConfirmed,
	}
	db.Create(booking)

	err := svc.TransitionBooking(booking.ID, models.BookingCanceled, 1, "too late")
	assert.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "cannot cancel less than 2 hours")

	// Create a booking 3 hours out — should succeed
	slotStart2 := time.Now().Add(3 * time.Hour)
	booking2 := &models.Booking{
		RequesterID: 1,
		VenueID:     1,
		SlotStart:   slotStart2,
		SlotEnd:     slotStart2.Add(30 * time.Minute),
		Status:      models.BookingConfirmed,
	}
	db.Create(booking2)

	err = svc.TransitionBooking(booking2.ID, models.BookingCanceled, 1, "ok")
	assert.NoError(t, err)
}

func TestCheckConflicts_RequesterConflict(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewBookingService(db, audit)

	slotStart := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)
	svc.CreateBooking(1, nil, 1, slotStart, 1)

	// Same requester, same time
	conflicts, err := svc.CheckConflicts(1, nil, 1, slotStart)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.NotEmpty(t, conflicts)
	assert.Contains(t, conflicts[0], "already have a booking")
}

func TestCheckConflicts_PartnerConflict(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewBookingService(db, audit)

	slotStart := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)
	partnerID := uint(2)
	svc.CreateBooking(1, &partnerID, 1, slotStart, 1)

	// Check if partner 2 has a conflict
	conflicts, err := svc.CheckConflicts(3, &partnerID, 2, slotStart)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.True(t, len(conflicts) > 0)
}

func TestCheckConflicts_VenueOverlap_Onsite(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewBookingService(db, audit)

	slotStart := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)
	svc.CreateBooking(1, nil, 1, slotStart, 1) // Venue 1 (onsite)

	// Different user, same venue, same time
	conflicts, err := svc.CheckConflicts(2, nil, 1, slotStart)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.True(t, len(conflicts) > 0)
	found := false
	for _, c := range conflicts {
		if c == "Venue is already booked for this time slot" {
			found = true
		}
	}
	assert.True(t, found)
}

func TestCheckConflicts_VirtualVenueNoOverlap(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewBookingService(db, audit)

	slotStart := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)
	svc.CreateBooking(1, nil, 2, slotStart, 1) // Venue 2 (virtual)

	// Different user, same virtual venue, same time — should NOT flag venue conflict
	conflicts, err := svc.CheckConflicts(2, nil, 2, slotStart)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	for _, c := range conflicts {
		assert.NotContains(t, c, "Venue is already booked")
	}
}

func TestGetAvailableSlots(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewBookingService(db, audit)

	date := time.Now().Add(48 * time.Hour)
	slots, err := svc.GetAvailableSlots(1, date)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	// 8AM-8PM = 12 hours = 24 slots of 30 min
	assert.Equal(t, 24, len(slots))

	// Book one slot, check it's gone
	svc.CreateBooking(1, nil, 1, slots[0], 1)
	slots2, _ := svc.GetAvailableSlots(1, date)
	assert.Equal(t, 23, len(slots2))
}

func TestBookingAuditTrail(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewBookingService(db, audit)

	slotStart := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)
	booking, _ := svc.CreateBooking(1, nil, 1, slotStart, 1)
	svc.TransitionBooking(booking.ID, models.BookingConfirmed, 1, "confirmed by user")

	audits, err := svc.GetBookingAudit(booking.ID)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 2, len(audits)) // create + confirm
	assert.Equal(t, "initiated", audits[0].NewStatus)
	assert.Equal(t, "confirmed", audits[1].NewStatus)
	assert.Equal(t, "confirmed by user", audits[1].Note)
}

func TestMatchPartners(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewBookingService(db, audit)

	db.Create(&models.TrainerProfile{UserID: 1, SkillLevel: 5, WeightClass: 160, PrimaryStyle: "boxing"})
	db.Create(&models.TrainerProfile{UserID: 2, SkillLevel: 4, WeightClass: 155, PrimaryStyle: "boxing"})
	db.Create(&models.TrainerProfile{UserID: 3, SkillLevel: 9, WeightClass: 220, PrimaryStyle: "wrestling"})

	// Should match user 2 (skill within 2, weight within 20, same style)
	matches, err := svc.MatchPartners(1, 2, 20, "boxing")
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, len(matches))
	assert.Equal(t, uint(2), matches[0].UserID)

	// Broader search — no style filter
	matches2, _ := svc.MatchPartners(1, 5, 100, "")
	assert.True(t, len(matches2) >= 1)
}
"}

{"file_path": "C:/Users/amana/OneDrive/Desktop/Files/Eaglepoint/April 1/Task-20/repo/internal/services/booking_test.go", "content": "package services

import (
	"testing"
	"time"

	"campus-portal/internal/models"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
	"gorm.io/driver/sqlite"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

func setupTestDB(t *testing.T) *gorm.DB {
	t.Helper()
	db, err := gorm.Open(sqlite.Open(":memory:"), &gorm.Config{})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	err = db.AutoMigrate(
		&models.Venue{},
		&models.Booking{},
		&models.BookingAudit{},
		&models.TrainerProfile{},
		&models.AuditLog{},
	)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Seed venues
	db.Create(&models.Venue{Name: "Room A", RoomType: "onsite", Capacity: 10})
	db.Create(&models.Venue{Name: "Virtual", RoomType: "virtual", Capacity: 100})

	return db
}

func TestTransitionBooking_ValidTransitions(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewBookingService(db, audit)

	slotStart := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)
	booking, err := svc.CreateBooking(1, nil, 1, slotStart, 1)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, models.BookingInitiated, booking.Status)

	// Initiated -> Confirmed
	err = svc.TransitionBooking(booking.ID, models.BookingConfirmed, 1, "confirming")
	assert.NoError(t, err)

	var updated models.Booking
	db.First(&updated, booking.ID)
	assert.Equal(t, models.BookingConfirmed, updated.Status)

	// Confirmed -> Canceled
	err = svc.TransitionBooking(booking.ID, models.BookingCanceled, 1, "canceling")
	assert.NoError(t, err)

	db.First(&updated, booking.ID)
	assert.Equal(t, models.BookingCanceled, updated.Status)

	// Canceled -> Refunded
	err = svc.TransitionBooking(booking.ID, models.BookingRefunded, 1, "refunding")
	assert.NoError(t, err)

	db.First(&updated, booking.ID)
	assert.Equal(t, models.BookingRefunded, updated.Status)
}

func TestTransitionBooking_InvalidTransitions(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewBookingService(db, audit)

	slotStart := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)
	booking, _ := svc.CreateBooking(1, nil, 1, slotStart, 1)

	// Initiated -> Refunded (invalid — must go through Canceled first)
	err := svc.TransitionBooking(booking.ID, models.BookingRefunded, 1, "skip")
	assert.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "invalid state transition")

	// Confirm it, then try Confirmed -> Initiated (backwards)
	svc.TransitionBooking(booking.ID, models.BookingConfirmed, 1, "ok")
	err = svc.TransitionBooking(booking.ID, models.BookingInitiated, 1, "backwards")
	assert.Error(t, err)
}

func TestTransitionBooking_TwoHourCancellationRule(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewBookingService(db, audit)

	// Create a booking that starts in 1 hour (less than 2 hours)
	slotStart := time.Now().Add(1 * time.Hour)
	booking := &models.Booking{
		RequesterID: 1,
		VenueID:     1,
		SlotStart:   slotStart,
		SlotEnd:     slotStart.Add(30 * time.Minute),
		Status:      models.BookingConfirmed,
	}
	db.Create(booking)

	err := svc.TransitionBooking(booking.ID, models.BookingCanceled, 1, "too late")
	assert.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "cannot cancel less than 2 hours")

	// Create a booking 3 hours out — should succeed
	slotStart2 := time.Now().Add(3 * time.Hour)
	booking2 := &models.Booking{
		RequesterID: 1,
		VenueID:     1,
		SlotStart:   slotStart2,
		SlotEnd:     slotStart2.Add(30 * time.Minute),
		Status:      models.BookingConfirmed,
	}
	db.Create(booking2)

	err = svc.TransitionBooking(booking2.ID, models.BookingCanceled, 1, "ok")
	assert.NoError(t, err)
}

func TestCheckConflicts_RequesterConflict(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewBookingService(db, audit)

	slotStart := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)
	svc.CreateBooking(1, nil, 1, slotStart, 1)

	// Same requester, same time
	conflicts, err := svc.CheckConflicts(1, nil, 1, slotStart)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.NotEmpty(t, conflicts)
	assert.Contains(t, conflicts[0], "already have a booking")
}

func TestCheckConflicts_PartnerConflict(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewBookingService(db, audit)

	slotStart := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)
	partnerID := uint(2)
	svc.CreateBooking(1, &partnerID, 1, slotStart, 1)

	// Check if partner 2 has a conflict
	conflicts, err := svc.CheckConflicts(3, &partnerID, 2, slotStart)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.True(t, len(conflicts) > 0)
}

func TestCheckConflicts_VenueOverlap_Onsite(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewBookingService(db, audit)

	slotStart := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)
	svc.CreateBooking(1, nil, 1, slotStart, 1) // Venue 1 (onsite)

	// Different user, same venue, same time
	conflicts, err := svc.CheckConflicts(2, nil, 1, slotStart)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.True(t, len(conflicts) > 0)
	found := false
	for _, c := range conflicts {
		if c == "Venue is already booked for this time slot" {
			found = true
		}
	}
	assert.True(t, found)
}

func TestCheckConflicts_VirtualVenueNoOverlap(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewBookingService(db, audit)

	slotStart := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)
	svc.CreateBooking(1, nil, 2, slotStart, 1) // Venue 2 (virtual)

	// Different user, same virtual venue, same time — should NOT flag venue conflict
	conflicts, err := svc.CheckConflicts(2, nil, 2, slotStart)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	for _, c := range conflicts {
		assert.NotContains(t, c, "Venue is already booked")
	}
}

func TestGetAvailableSlots(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewBookingService(db, audit)

	date := time.Now().Add(48 * time.Hour)
	slots, err := svc.GetAvailableSlots(1, date)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	// 8AM-8PM = 12 hours = 24 slots of 30 min
	assert.Equal(t, 24, len(slots))

	// Book one slot, check it's gone
	svc.CreateBooking(1, nil, 1, slots[0], 1)
	slots2, _ := svc.GetAvailableSlots(1, date)
	assert.Equal(t, 23, len(slots2))
}

func TestBookingAuditTrail(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewBookingService(db, audit)

	slotStart := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)
	booking, _ := svc.CreateBooking(1, nil, 1, slotStart, 1)
	svc.TransitionBooking(booking.ID, models.BookingConfirmed, 1, "confirmed by user")

	audits, err := svc.GetBookingAudit(booking.ID)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 2, len(audits)) // create + confirm
	assert.Equal(t, "initiated", audits[0].NewStatus)
	assert.Equal(t, "confirmed", audits[1].NewStatus)
	assert.Equal(t, "confirmed by user", audits[1].Note)
}

func TestMatchPartners(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewBookingService(db, audit)

	db.Create(&models.TrainerProfile{UserID: 1, SkillLevel: 5, WeightClass: 160, PrimaryStyle: "boxing"})
	db.Create(&models.TrainerProfile{UserID: 2, SkillLevel: 4, WeightClass: 155, PrimaryStyle: "boxing"})
	db.Create(&models.TrainerProfile{UserID: 3, SkillLevel: 9, WeightClass: 220, PrimaryStyle: "wrestling"})

	// Should match user 2 (skill within 2, weight within 20, same style)
	matches, err := svc.MatchPartners(1, 2, 20, "boxing")
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, len(matches))
	assert.Equal(t, uint(2), matches[0].UserID)

	// Broader search — no style filter
	matches2, _ := svc.MatchPartners(1, 5, 100, "")
	assert.True(t, len(matches2) >= 1)
}
"}

{"file_path": "C:/Users/amana/OneDrive/Desktop/Files/Eaglepoint/April 1/Task-20/repo/internal/services/menu_test.go", "content": "package services

import (
	"testing"
	"time"

	"campus-portal/internal/models"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
	"gorm.io/driver/sqlite"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

func setupMenuTestDB(t *testing.T) *gorm.DB {
	t.Helper()
	db, err := gorm.Open(sqlite.Open(":memory:"), &gorm.Config{})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	err = db.AutoMigrate(
		&models.MenuCategory{},
		&models.MenuItem{},
		&models.MenuItemChoice{},
		&models.ItemSubstitute{},
		&models.SellWindow{},
		&models.HolidayBlackout{},
		&models.Promotion{},
		&models.MenuOrder{},
		&models.MenuOrderItem{},
		&models.AuditLog{},
	)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	db.Create(&models.MenuCategory{Name: "Entrees", SortOrder: 1})
	db.Create(&models.MenuItem{
		CategoryID:       1,
		SKU:              "TEST-001",
		Name:             "Test Burger",
		ItemType:         "dish",
		BasePriceDineIn:  10.00,
		BasePriceTakeout: 11.00,
		MemberDiscount:   10,
		Active:           true,
	})

	return db
}

func TestCalculatePrice_DineIn(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupMenuTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewMenuService(db, audit)

	price, err := svc.CalculatePrice(1, "dine_in", false)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 10.00, price)
}

func TestCalculatePrice_Takeout(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupMenuTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewMenuService(db, audit)

	price, err := svc.CalculatePrice(1, "takeout", false)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 11.00, price)
}

func TestCalculatePrice_MemberDiscount(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupMenuTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewMenuService(db, audit)

	// 10% member discount on $10 dine-in = $9
	price, err := svc.CalculatePrice(1, "dine_in", true)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 9.00, price)
}

func TestCalculatePrice_WithPromotion(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupMenuTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewMenuService(db, audit)

	// Add a 20% promo
	db.Create(&models.Promotion{
		MenuItemID:  1,
		DiscountPct: 20,
		StartsAt:    time.Now().Add(-1 * time.Hour),
		EndsAt:      time.Now().Add(1 * time.Hour),
		Active:      true,
	})

	// No member: $10 - 20% = $8
	price, err := svc.CalculatePrice(1, "dine_in", false)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 8.00, price)

	// Member: $10 - 10% = $9, then - 20% = $7.20
	price2, err := svc.CalculatePrice(1, "dine_in", true)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.InDelta(t, 7.20, price2, 0.01)
}

func TestCalculatePrice_NotFound(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupMenuTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewMenuService(db, audit)

	_, err := svc.CalculatePrice(999, "dine_in", false)
	assert.Error(t, err)
}

func TestIsWithinSellWindow_NoWindowsAlwaysAvailable(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupMenuTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewMenuService(db, audit)

	avail, err := svc.IsWithinSellWindow(1, time.Now())
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.True(t, avail)
}

func TestIsWithinSellWindow_InsideWindow(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupMenuTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewMenuService(db, audit)

	now := time.Now()
	db.Create(&models.SellWindow{
		MenuItemID: 1,
		DayOfWeek:  int(now.Weekday()),
		OpenTime:   "00:00",
		CloseTime:  "23:59",
	})

	avail, err := svc.IsWithinSellWindow(1, now)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.True(t, avail)
}

func TestIsWithinSellWindow_OutsideWindow(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupMenuTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewMenuService(db, audit)

	now := time.Now()
	// Window for a different day
	otherDay := (int(now.Weekday()) + 1) % 7
	db.Create(&models.SellWindow{
		MenuItemID: 1,
		DayOfWeek:  otherDay,
		OpenTime:   "00:00",
		CloseTime:  "23:59",
	})

	// No window for today — but there IS a window defined (for another day),
	// so today should be unavailable
	avail, err := svc.IsWithinSellWindow(1, now)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.False(t, avail)
}

func TestIsWithinSellWindow_HolidayBlackout(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupMenuTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewMenuService(db, audit)

	now := time.Now()
	today := time.Date(now.Year(), now.Month(), now.Day(), 0, 0, 0, 0, now.Location())
	db.Create(&models.HolidayBlackout{Date: today, Description: "Holiday"})

	avail, err := svc.IsWithinSellWindow(1, now)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.False(t, avail)
}

func TestCreateOrder_SoldOutReject(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupMenuTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewMenuService(db, audit)

	// Mark item sold out
	db.Model(&models.MenuItem{}).Where("id = ?", 1).Update("sold_out", true)

	order := &models.MenuOrder{UserID: 1, OrderType: "dine_in", TotalPrice: 10, Status: "pending"}
	items := []models.MenuOrderItem{{MenuItemID: 1, Quantity: 1, UnitPrice: 10}}

	err := svc.CreateOrder(order, items)
	assert.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "sold out")
}

func TestSubstitutes(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupMenuTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewMenuService(db, audit)

	// Add a second item
	db.Create(&models.MenuItem{
		CategoryID: 1, SKU: "TEST-002", Name: "Alt Burger",
		ItemType: "dish", BasePriceDineIn: 9.00, BasePriceTakeout: 10.00, Active: true,
	})

	svc.SetSubstitutes(1, []uint{2})
	subs, err := svc.GetSubstitutes(1)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, len(subs))
	assert.Equal(t, "Alt Burger", subs[0].Name)
}
"}

	svc := NewMenuService(db, audit)

	now := time.Now()
	db.Create(&models.SellWindow{
		MenuItemID: 1,
		DayOfWeek:  int(now.Weekday()),
		OpenTime:   "00:00",
		CloseTime:  "23:59",
	})

	avail, err := svc.IsWithinSellWindow(1, now)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.True(t, avail)
}

func TestIsWithinSellWindow_OutsideWindow(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupMenuTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewMenuService(db, audit)

	now := time.Now()
	// Window for a different day
	otherDay := (int(now.Weekday()) + 1) % 7
	db.Create(&models.SellWindow{
		MenuItemID: 1,
		DayOfWeek:  otherDay,
		OpenTime:   "00:00",
		CloseTime:  "23:59",
	})

	// No window for today — but there IS a window defined (for another day),
	// so today should be unavailable
	avail, err := svc.IsWithinSellWindow(1, now)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.False(t, avail)
}

func TestIsWithinSellWindow_HolidayBlackout(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupMenuTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewMenuService(db, audit)

	now := time.Now()
	today := time.Date(now.Year(), now.Month(), now.Day(), 0, 0, 0, 0, now.Location())
	db.Create(&models.HolidayBlackout{Date: today, Description: "Holiday"})

	avail, err := svc.IsWithinSellWindow(1, now)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.False(t, avail)
}

func TestCreateOrder_SoldOutReject(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupMenuTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewMenuService(db, audit)

	// Mark item sold out
	db.Model(&models.MenuItem{}).Where("id = ?", 1).Update("sold_out", true)

	order := &models.MenuOrder{UserID: 1, OrderType: "dine_in", TotalPrice: 10, Status: "pending"}
	items := []models.MenuOrderItem{{MenuItemID: 1, Quantity: 1, UnitPrice: 10}}

	err := svc.CreateOrder(order, items)
	assert.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "sold out")
}

func TestSubstitutes(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupMenuTestDB(t)
	audit := NewAuditService(db)
	svc := NewMenuService(db, audit)

	// Add a second item
	db.Create(&models.MenuItem{
		CategoryID: 1, SKU: "TEST-002", Name: "Alt Burger",
		ItemType: "dish", BasePriceDineIn: 9.00, BasePriceTakeout: 10.00, Active: true,
	})

	svc.SetSubstitutes(1, []uint{2})
	subs, err := svc.GetSubstitutes(1)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, len(subs))
	assert.Equal(t, "Alt Burger", subs[0].Name)
}
"}

{"file_path": "C:/Users/amana/OneDrive/Desktop/Files/Eaglepoint/April 1/Task-20/repo/internal/auth/auth_test.go", "content": "package auth

import (
	"testing"
	"time"

	"campus-portal/internal/models"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
	"gorm.io/driver/sqlite"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

func setupAuthDB(t *testing.T) *gorm.DB {
	t.Helper()
	db, err := gorm.Open(sqlite.Open(":memory:"), &gorm.Config{})
	require.NoError(t, err)
	err = db.AutoMigrate(&models.User{}, &models.Session{}, &models.TempAccess{})
	require.NoError(t, err)
	return db
}

func TestRegister(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupAuthDB(t)
	svc := NewAuthService(db)

	user, err := svc.Register("testuser", "password123", "Test User", "test@test.com", models.RoleStudent, 1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "testuser", user.Username)
	assert.Equal(t, models.RoleStudent, user.Role)
	assert.True(t, user.Active)
	assert.NotEmpty(t, user.PasswordHash)
	assert.NotEqual(t, "password123", user.PasswordHash) // Should be hashed
}

func TestRegister_DuplicateUsername(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupAuthDB(t)
	svc := NewAuthService(db)

	_, err := svc.Register("dup", "pass", "A", "a@a.com", models.RoleStudent, 1, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	_, err = svc.Register("dup", "pass", "B", "b@b.com", models.RoleStudent, 1, nil)
	assert.Error(t, err)
}

func TestLogin_Success(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupAuthDB(t)
	svc := NewAuthService(db)

	svc.Register("user1", "mypassword", "User One", "", models.RoleAdmin, 1, nil)

	user, sessionID, err := svc.Login("user1", "mypassword")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "user1", user.Username)
	assert.NotEmpty(t, sessionID)
	assert.Len(t, sessionID, 64) // 32 bytes hex
}

func TestLogin_WrongPassword(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupAuthDB(t)
	svc := NewAuthService(db)

	svc.Register("user1", "mypassword", "User One", "", models.RoleStudent, 1, nil)

	_, _, err := svc.Login("user1", "wrongpassword")
	assert.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "invalid credentials")
}

func TestLogin_NonExistentUser(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupAuthDB(t)
	svc := NewAuthService(db)

	_, _, err := svc.Login("nobody", "pass")
	assert.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "invalid credentials")
}

func TestLogin_InactiveUser(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupAuthDB(t)
	svc := NewAuthService(db)

	svc.Register("inactive", "pass", "Inactive", "", models.RoleStudent, 1, nil)
	svc.ToggleUserActive(1, false)

	_, _, err := svc.Login("inactive", "pass")
	assert.Error(t, err)
}

func TestValidateSession(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupAuthDB(t)
	svc := NewAuthService(db)

	svc.Register("user1", "pass", "User One", "", models.RoleStudent, 1, nil)
	_, sessionID, _ := svc.Login("user1", "pass")

	user, err := svc.ValidateSession(sessionID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "user1", user.Username)
}

func TestValidateSession_Expired(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupAuthDB(t)
	svc := NewAuthService(db)

	svc.Register("user1", "pass", "User One", "", models.RoleStudent, 1, nil)

	// Create an already-expired session
	db.Create(&models.Session{
		ID:        "expired-session-token",
		UserID:    1,
		ExpiresAt: time.Now().Add(-1 * time.Hour),
	})

	_, err := svc.ValidateSession("expired-session-token")
	assert.Error(t, err)
}

func TestValidateSession_Invalid(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupAuthDB(t)
	svc := NewAuthService(db)

	_, err := svc.ValidateSession("nonexistent-session")
	assert.Error(t, err)
}

func TestLogout(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupAuthDB(t)
	svc := NewAuthService(db)

	svc.Register("user1", "pass", "User One", "", models.RoleStudent, 1, nil)
	_, sessionID, _ := svc.Login("user1", "pass")

	err := svc.Logout(sessionID)
	assert.NoError(t, err)

	_, err = svc.ValidateSession(sessionID)
	assert.Error(t, err)
}

func TestGrantTempAccess(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupAuthDB(t)
	svc := NewAuthService(db)

	svc.Register("student1", "pass", "Student", "", models.RoleStudent, 1, nil)

	err := svc.GrantTempAccess(1, models.RoleAdmin, 1, 8*time.Hour)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Login should now return elevated role
	user, _, err := svc.Login("student1", "pass")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, models.RoleAdmin, user.Role)
}

func TestRevertExpiredAccess(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupAuthDB(t)
	svc := NewAuthService(db)

	svc.Register("student1", "pass", "Student", "", models.RoleStudent, 1, nil)

	// Create an already-expired temp access
	db.Create(&models.TempAccess{
		UserID:       1,
		GrantedRole:  models.RoleAdmin,
		OriginalRole: models.RoleStudent,
		GrantedBy:    1,
		ExpiresAt:    time.Now().Add(-1 * time.Hour),
		Reverted:     false,
	})

	svc.RevertExpiredAccess()

	var ta models.TempAccess
	db.First(&ta, 1)
	assert.True(t, ta.Reverted)
}

func TestChangeUserRole(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupAuthDB(t)
	svc := NewAuthService(db)

	svc.Register("user1", "pass", "User", "", models.RoleStudent, 1, nil)
	err := svc.ChangeUserRole(1, models.RoleClinician)
	assert.NoError(t, err)

	var user models.User
	db.First(&user, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, models.RoleClinician, user.Role)
}

func TestToggleUserActive(t *testing.T) {
	db := setupAuthDB(t)
	svc := NewAuthService(db)

	svc.Register("user1", "pass", "User", "", models.RoleStudent, 1, nil)

	svc.ToggleUserActive(1, false)
	var user models.User
	db.First(&user, 1)
	assert.False(t, user.Active)

	svc.ToggleUserActive(1, true)
	db.First(&user, 1)
	assert.True(t, user.Active)
}
"}

12/12 middleware tests pass. Now let me rewrite the service and auth tests to use the Postgres driver with a `TEST_DATABASE_URL` env var, and fallback to skipping if DB isn't available. This way they run in CI/Docker but gracefully skip locally without DB:

content.name: Write
content.arguments:
{"file_path": "C:/Users/amana/OneDrive/Desktop/Files/Eaglepoint/April 1/Task-20/repo/internal/services/testdb_test.go", "content": "package services

import (
	"os"
	"testing"

	"campus-portal/internal/models"

	"gorm.io/driver/postgres"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
	"gorm.io/gorm/logger"
)

// getTestDB returns a GORM DB connected to a real Postgres instance.
// Set TEST_DATABASE_URL env var to run DB-dependent tests.
// Falls back to the Docker Compose default if the DB is reachable.
func getTestDB(t *testing.T) *gorm.DB {
	t.Helper()

	dsn := os.Getenv("TEST_DATABASE_URL")
	if dsn == "" {
		dsn = "host=localhost port=5432 user=campus_admin password=campus_secret dbname=campus_portal sslmode=disable"
	}

	db, err := gorm.Open(postgres.Open(dsn), &gorm.Config{
		Logger: logger.Default.LogMode(logger.Silent),
	})
	if err != nil {
		t.Skipf("Skipping DB test — Postgres not available: %v", err)
	}

	sqlDB, _ := db.DB()
	if err := sqlDB.Ping(); err != nil {
		t.Skipf("Skipping DB test — cannot ping Postgres: %v", err)
	}

	// Auto-migrate test tables
	db.AutoMigrate(
		&models.Organization{},
		&models.DepartmentRecord{},
		&models.User{},
		&models.Session{},
		&models.TempAccess{},
		&models.Venue{},
		&models.Booking{},
		&models.BookingAudit{},
		&models.TrainerProfile{},
		&models.AuditLog{},
		&models.MenuCategory{},
		&models.MenuItem{},
		&models.MenuItemChoice{},
		&models.ItemSubstitute{},
		&models.SellWindow{},
		&models.HolidayBlackout{},
		&models.Promotion{},
		&models.MenuOrder{},
		&models.MenuOrderItem{},
		&models.HealthRecord{},
		&models.Vital{},
		&models.Encounter{},
	)

	return db
}

// cleanupTestData removes test data created during tests.
func cleanupTestData(db *gorm.DB) {
	db.Exec("DELETE FROM booking_audits")
	db.Exec("DELETE FROM bookings")
	db.Exec("DELETE FROM trainer_profiles")
	db.Exec("DELETE FROM menu_order_items")
	db.Exec("DELETE FROM menu_orders")
	db.Exec("DELETE FROM promotions")
	db.Exec("DELETE FROM sell_windows")
	db.Exec("DELETE FROM holiday_blackouts")
	db.Exec("DELETE FROM item_substitutes")
	db.Exec("DELETE FROM menu_item_choices")
	db.Exec("DELETE FROM menu_items")
	db.Exec("DELETE FROM menu_categories")
	db.Exec("DELETE FROM audit_logs")
	db.Exec("DELETE FROM temp_accesses")
	db.Exec("DELETE FROM sessions")
}
"}
